questions.question: What best describes supervised learning?
questions.explanation: Supervised learning trains on labeled examples to map inputs to known outputs.

questions.question: Which metric is often important when missing a true positive is costly?
questions.explanation: Recall measures how many true positives are captured.

questions.question: Why keep a separate test set?
options: To estimate generalization on unseen data
questions.explanation: The test set gives a more realistic estimate of performance on new data.

questions.question: Feature engineering mainly helps by:
options: Reducing all data to one column
questions.explanation: Better features often improve model signal and performance.

questions.question: What is a common sign of overfitting?
options: Low train and low test performance
High train performance and low test performance
Equal train/test performance with low variance
questions.explanation: Overfitting occurs when a model learns training details that do not generalize.

questions.question: Validation data is primarily used to:
questions.explanation: Validation guides model selection before final testing.

questions.question: Dropout in neural networks is used to:
options: Reduce overfitting by random neuron deactivation during training

questions.question: Model drift refers to:
options: A shift where real-world data patterns change over time
questions.explanation: Drift can degrade deployed model performance and requires monitoring.

questions.question: Which is a key post-deployment practice?
options: Monitoring performance and retraining when needed
questions.explanation: Monitoring and retraining maintain production quality.

questions.question: Which option is NOT a typical ML workflow stage?
questions.explanation: Skipping validation usually leads to poor reliability.
